result:
Successfully cleaned and preprocessed the survey datasets. The R script:

1. Loaded all 4 waves from data/input/ using read_delim with tab delimiter
2. Merged datasets using full_join by NSID (the original ID variable)
3. Recoded wave-specific missing values to standard codes:
   - -999 → -2 (Information lost)
   - -99, -98 → -3 (Not asked)
   - -94 → -8 (Insufficient information)
   - -96 → -1 (Item not applicable)
   - -92 → -9 (Refusal)
   - NA → -3 (Not asked)
4. Created labeled factor variables with explicit labels for all categories
5. Generated age-specific parental economic activity variables:
   - ecoactdtma14, ecoactdtma15, ecoactdtma16, ecoactdtma17 (mother)
   - ecoactdtpa14, ecoactdtpa15, ecoactdtpa16, ecoactdtpa17 (father)
6. Output cleaned_data.csv to data/output/ with 16,111 rows and 9 columns

The output file contains the NSID identifier and 8 harmonized parental economic activity variables with consistent categories across all ages (Full-time work, Part-time work, Unemployed, Training, Education, Looking after home, Retired, Sick/disabled, Other) plus properly labeled missing value codes.
